newsletters.subject: Hermes Agent.
newsletters.reason: Aakash Gupta (AI by Aakash) newsletter about Hermes Agent by Nous Research — the same system running this pipeline. Covers SOUL.md persistent memory, learning loop, setup/install, mobile integration, weekly briefings, health coaching, ComfyUI skill. Also includes weekly AI news: Claude's creative tool integration (Blender/Fusion/Adobe/Ableton), Gemini vs Copilot, OpenAI/Microsoft AGI clause removal, Cursor SDK, Grok Imagine Agent.

newsletters.subject: DeepSeek v4, and the end of the OpenAI/Microsoft AGI clause
newsletters.reason: Simon Willison's newsletter covering: (1) DeepSeek V4 release — 1.6T MoE, 1M context, MIT license, dramatically lower cost vs GPT-5.5/Gemini. (2) End of OpenAI/Microsoft AGI clause — legal restructuring of partnership through 2032. (3) LLM 0.32a0 library major refactor — multi-modal, streaming, reasoning tokens. (4) Industry trends: Zig anti-AI policy, VibeVoice, Talkie-1930, pip lockfiles.

newsletters.subject: [AINews] AI Engineer World's Fair — Autoresearch, Memory, World Models, Tokenmaxxing, Agentic Commerce, and Vertic…
newsletters.reason: swyx's AINews — dense with technical and industry developments. Covers: AI Engineer World's Fair 2026 new tracks (Autoresearch, Memory, World Models, Agentic Commerce, Vertical AI, Robotics), Grok 4.3 (40% cheaper, Intelligence Index 53), DeepSeek V4 Pro/Vision specs, GPT-5.5/Codex ($1.73 for 12h cyber task in 11min), ReaLM-Retrieve (10.1% F1 improvement), OCR-Memory, recursive multi-agent systems, PFlash (10x llama.cpp speedup), Qwen-Scope SAEs, Spark Cluster builds, Google TPU 8t/8i. Also covers industry trends: durable execution, open vs closed gap narrowing, GPT 'goblin' phenomenon.
